Eval 25 covers the v1.14 dedup + already-addressed logic: copilot[bot] posts identical review body and timeline comments (should dedup to one), and @alice posts a question that the PR author answers with an @mention (should be marked already addressed). with_skill 4/4 (100%), without_skill 1/4 (25%) — strongly discriminating. The baseline recognizes the @alice exchange as addressed (explicit in prompt) but lacks the formal 200-char dedup rule that keeps the review body version and discards the timeline duplicate.

Eval 26 covers outdated-thread-concern-persists: strong discriminator (+1.00) — default LLM auto-skips isOutdated=true threads without verification; skill reads current file and classifies as fix if concern persists.
Eval 27 covers outdated-thread-concern-addressed: partial discriminator (+0.67) — default LLM skips for wrong reason (flag not verified read); skill explicitly reads file before confirming skip.
